title: F6 composed checking
manager: Verify a whole system without the state-space product blowing up — each member proves its interface contract once, and the system composes those contracts instead of exploring every combined state. This is what makes the five-hundred-machine park verifiable: it becomes one proof per member plus a composition argument, rather than an intractable product of five hundred state spaces that no checker could ever finish.
cs: finite-system = members finite + population max + queue bound (compiler-enforced); checks bound safety, lifted contracts, deliverability, invariant over count/all/any/sum, opt-in deadlock; the compositional scaling story.
